description: Single source of truth for agent permissions. The agent enforces these rules at runtime (emitting permission_check spans); the CottonMouth backend serves them to the governance UI. Deny-by-default: anything outside an allow rule is blocked.

llm_gateway.description: Model access, budgets, and rate limits are enforced by the shared in-cluster LiteLLM gateway via virtual keys — NOT duplicated in CottonMouth. CottonMouth observes the gateway's decisions (recording denials as governance events) and reconciles each agent's declared model access against the entitlements on its actual virtual key (drift detection).
llm_gateway.reconcile: CottonMouth reads /key/info and /v1/models from the gateway to surface each agent's allowed models, budget, and spend alongside its CottonMouth tool permissions.

gateway-assistant.description: Q&A agent that routes every model call through the shared LiteLLM gateway (no direct provider creds). Its model access is enforced by the gateway; CottonMouth reconciles the declared set against what the gateway actually exposes.
gateway-assistant.enforcement: LiteLLM gateway (virtual key); CottonMouth observes + reconciles

devils-council.description: External Cursor agents that route every model call through the shared LiteLLM gateway via their team virtual key (alias 'devils-council'). CottonMouth observes the model traffic, cost, and the gateway's access decisions; it does not see Cursor's internal reasoning/tool steps unless those are MCP-routed through the gateway.
devils-council.enforcement: LiteLLM gateway (virtual key); CottonMouth observes + reconciles

ops-assistant.description: Tool-using Bedrock agent that reads/writes files, runs shell commands, and fetches URLs inside a sandboxed workspace.
ops-assistant.enforcement: CottonMouth SDK permission_check spans, evaluated before every tool call

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create or overwrite a text file

rules.description: File reads and writes must stay within the sandbox workspace.
Shell binaries the agent is permitted to execute.
Destructive or unpermitted binaries that are always blocked.
Hosts the agent is permitted to make HTTP requests to.
